items.title: PixVerse's $2B valuation shows investors still believe AI video generation has room for another winner
items.summary: Singapore-based AI video startup PixVerse is now valued at over $2 billion after an extended Series C round. The article PixVerse's $2B valuation shows investors still believe AI video generation has room for another winner appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: Google Search now generates AI images when it can't find what you're looking for on the web
items.summary: Google is adding AI image generation to Search's AI Overviews. When no matching image exists on the web, the new Nano Banana 2 Lite model generates one from the search query. The rollout starts in the coming weeks. The article Google Search now generates AI images when it can't f

items.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
items.summary: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity costs, water supplies, or local control.

items.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
items.summary: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

items.title: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
items.summary: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly want open models, due to cost, accessibility, and ownership. Do frontier models still matter if most production AI ends up running on open models?

items.title: ChatGPT returns to WhatsApp in Europe after EU forces Meta to open the door to rival AI bots
items.summary: OpenAI has re-enabled ChatGPT on WhatsApp, but only in the European Economic Area, covering the 27 EU member states plus Liechtenstein, Iceland, and Norway. The article ChatGPT returns to WhatsApp in Europe after EU forces Meta to open the door to rival AI bots appeared first on

items.title: Deepmind CEO Hassabis says "nobody in the world knows what happens next" so "cautious optimism" means building guardrails now
items.summary: Google Deepmind CEO Demis Hassabis has published a sweeping proposal for how to handle advanced AI. He wants a new US standards body modeled after financial regulator FINRA that would develop evaluation protocols for frontier models and could coordinate a slowdown in AI developme

items.title: Claude responds with more warmth in Hindi and more rigor in Russian, showing how language shapes AI answers

items.title: Google revamps image search for its 25th anniversary with more images and more AI
items.summary: The new Google image search will use your "unique interests" to create an always-updated gallery.

items.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
items.summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools.

items.title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
items.summary: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.

items.title: Superhuman’s new auto-draft feature almost makes me like AI replies
items.summary: Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.

items.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
items.summary: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.
